name: Deviled Ham (Spread for Bread)
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups cooked ham, see noted below
1 1/2 tablespoons coarse mustard, see noted below
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim ham of any gristle or membranes. Use trimmings or cubes.
2. Measure the ham fat.
3. Picnic hams are suitable for making deviled ham.
4. Choose a well-flavored vinegar (white wine vinegar keeps the color light, balsamic adds flavor).
5. Select strong-flavored or horseradish mustards; avoid yellow turmeric mustards.
6. Use your preferred hot pepper sauce and add more or less to taste (Habanero sauces are good, but jalapeno pepper rings can be used instead).
7. Pulse the ham and ham fat in a food processor until coarsely chopped.
8. Add remaining ingredients to the food processor bowl and pulse until a coarse pate is achieved.
9. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed by pulsing a few more times for a finer texture.
10. Pack the spread into jars or tight plastic containers and store in the refrigerator. The flavors will develop over the first day or two, then it will keep for about 2 weeks.
